Key Takeaways:
• This lesson is about creating your PLC3.
• PLC2 is the Ownership video – the how. Have them feel what it’s like to own the
transformation and to own your product.
• Open with a thanks for the comments and questions from PLC2 and show
excitement.
• Recap is important – esp. w/a JV launch where often people are only mailed
directly into your PLC3 video.
• Thanking them for comments gives social proof.
• Then you need to teach valuable content – do a case study if you’ve got one – or
tell your own story as your own case study.
• Then answer the top questions you’ve been getting – answer top objections.
• Give the big view- the big picture.
• Pivot to your offer and create a soft landing. Let them know something’s coming-
create a soft landing for your offer in your next video.
• Seed the scarcity of your offer.
• Download the PLC3 Quickstart Guide under the video for sample language to use,
tips for creating your PLC, and a PLC3 checklist.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

Okay, PLC3, this is where we're bringing it home.
This is what we call "The Ownership Experience," or frankly I'm starting to just
call it "Ownership." This is the Ownership video.
So, here we are in our overall map. Talked about this already a few times, we
have the Pre-Pre, then the PLC1, PLC2, now we're on PLC3. Another way to think about
this, well it's The Opportunity, The Transformation, The Ownership Experience.
Sometimes at PLC1 I call it "The Journey." So it could be The Journey, The
Transformation, and then Ownership or Opportunity –Transformation- Ownership.
Another way I like to think about this is why, for PLC1, what, for PLC2, and how, how to,
for PLC3. That's actually what I just said right there, it why, what, and now how.
So you've shown in the first two pieces that potential transformation or change
that your avatar can have. You know, they're moving towards more pleasure, or away
from pain. No matter what your selling, okay. The ultimate answer of this whole journey
through the PLC is to buy your product. By the end of PLC3, they're going to see that.
That's what you want. First, you really have to continue to build value, and that's a key
thing in PLC3. This is all about having them feel what it's like to own that
transformation, and to own your product. That's why they call it "Ownership."
So here's the framework, the overall recipe for PLC3. It starts off with thanking
them, and showing excitement. Thank them for the comments and the questions from
PLC2.
So this very similar to PLC2, where you're thanking them for their reaction to
PLC1. So now, as I talked about with PLC2, you have sort of a couple different universes.
You have people that this is the first video that they're going to watch. Then you have
people that have seen PLC1 and 2. You have some people that have just seen PLC2. You
have some people that have just seen PLC1. So they're going to be in all different places.
When you have an Internal Launch, even then, where you're mailing your list,
your avatar about each piece, you have a little more control. Once you get into a Joint
Venture Launch, where you have partners, and some of them - PLC3 is going to be the
first piece that they mail on. So it gets a little more chaotic as you get to PLC3, so you just
have to keep that in mind. That some people, even if they've clicked through to the first

© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
two pieces of Prelaunch content, they might not have consumed them. So you always
have to remember that.
So first of all, you thank them. Even if they haven't seen those first ones, that
thanking for those comments and those reactions, that's building social proof. Then just
like in PLC2, you want to have a quick recap of the opportunity and your positioning.
You don't want to go on, and on, and on, and belabor it, but you have to talk about that
journey and that opportunity really quickly. Remind them why they should be listening,
and "Oh, by the way. Who are you, and why should they pay attention to you?" So you
have to move through this quickly, because for someone who has seen everything up
until now, it'll feel repetitive if you belabor it and go too long on this. By the way, if you
can do this in a slightly different way than you did in PLC2, so much the better.
Now this is a great place to drop in a case study of you've got one, and if you're
your only case study, then you can drop in yourself as your own case study.
Then you want answer the top questions you've been getting. In other words,
you're going to answer those top objections. I've talked about this extensively when I was
talking about PLC2. So if you missed that one, go back and watch the video where I
talked about PLC2 and walk you through this. But you want to answer those questions,
and often you can ... People will ask the same question, or raise the same objections in
different ways with different questions. So go ahead, and you can answer them, and you
can answer slightly different questions that still get to the same objection.
Okay, so this video really often is, this piece of Prelaunch content is often, it's
about the big view. So when I do it, when I'm going through my launch for Product
Launch Formula, this is my Product Launch Blueprint. A Product Launch Blueprint sort
of gives the overall view of the entire process. Every bit of the launch process, where I
just walk through and I name it. I name it, I talk a little bit about it. Isn't like I go
through great super deep detail about every piece, because well, every piece is Product
Launch Formula. As you know, Product Launch Formula is this big thing. You're,
whatever, 80% of the way through this course and you know what, there's a lot here.
My Product Launch Blueprint video isn't nowhere near the depth that I go in
here, but I name almost all the different pieces. In naming those pieces, people, they
start to get a feeling for what it's like for me to teach them. For them to have mastery of
all those different pieces. That's when I talk about the ownership. What it would feel like
to own Product Launch Formula. More importantly, what would it be like to own the
mastery of this process? What would it feel like to own the end results of this process?
The end result of having a successful launch. That's what I'm teaching when I do my

PLC3, the Product Launch Blueprint.
You could be doing the same thing. Whether it's having this great meditation
practice, or being able to play the guitar, or being able to take great digital photos and
publish them. Any one of those, you can show what it's like to be the person who owns
that. Owns your product, own the experience, owns the mastery of that process of
whatever you're teaching. The mastery of being a non-smoker. Have them own that
experience. That's what you're doing in PLC3.
Now one the big things here, is bullet number six. You have to do this. This is the
mistake, in the very first version of Product Launch Formula, and even in the second
version, I didn't teach hard enough on this topic. So this is a mistake people used to
make. I don't see my PLF Owners make it anymore, because I hammer in this. It’s
really important to pivot to your offer, and create a soft landing. Now what
do I mean by that? In the last 25% or so of your video, somewhere near the end of your
PLC3, you have to let people know something's coming. This creates a soft landing for
your offer in your next video.
Because by now, they're too ... Almost all the way through your three videos that
have been teaching them. You've been building this relationship. You've been delivering
value. You've been doing an amazing job with this, and they've fallen in love with you.
But now, you're going to ask them in the next video to buy from you. It's like, you don't
want it to be, "I'm your best friend, I'm your best friend, I'm your best friend, I'm your
best friend. We're awesome, we're awesome. We love each other. Oh, buy from me. Buy
my stuff, buy my stuff, buy my stuff." So you have to create a transition in here. You have
to create a transition, and it's very simple. It's very simple.
It's something along these lines…
"Hey, I've loved sharing these videos. I've loved creating these videos for you.
I've gotten so many great comments, so much feedback. It's so exciting for me to see
everyone really getting this, and how big of deal this is, and the impact it can make on
your life and the life of those around you, and what it can do for your future. And it's
just been fantastic sharing this. I've loved this process. I've loved this interaction we've
had. I've loved getting all the comments from you.
Now in my next video, what I'm going to be sharing is how to go even deeper,
because I'm going to be opening up a limited program, and I'm going to be taking on a
number of clients, a number of students, that want to go deeper.
www.getwsodo.com	www.getwsodo.com

6
© 2017 Internet Alchemy, Inc. I www.ProductLaunchFormula.com
And so I've created this program, which is basically the next step beyond this. So
if you're ready to move forward. If you're ready to really get mastery in this area,
really change your life, to transform your life. Then I can't wait to share, and I'm so
excited to share this program I put together. And I'll be sharing that with you in my
next video."
…So it's something along that lines. You definitely need to put that in towards the
end of your third video, so you have this transition to the sale.
Now one the things you want to know ... Bullet seven here. You want to seed the
scarcity of your launch offer. This is something that every launch offer ... There has to be
a beginning and an end to your launch. At the end to your launch, if they don't decide to
act and jump on board with you, there has to be some negative consequence. Either the
price goes up, or certain bonuses go away, or the product comes off the market. Those
are the three primary ways to get scarcity, and I'll repeat those. Either the price goes up,
bonuses go away, or the product comes off the market. You could have more than one of
those. You could use more ... It could be, the price goes up and bonuses go away. Or
bonuses go away and product goes off the market. Or it could be all three of those, but at
least one of those three things has to be true at the end of your launch to get people to
move. You want to create a deadline, and you want to mention that in this PLC3. That
you only have ... There's going to be a limited window to jump on board.
In that last bullet I was talking about pivoting the offer, and it could be, "I've got
this program coming up. I'm going to be working with a limited number of clients, so
we're only going to be having this registration period open for a certain period of time,
for a limited period of time. So you're going to want to jump ... You're going to want to
check out that next video right away." Or it could be, you know there I just said, "A
limited number of clients." It could be, "I'm only going to have that registration window
open for a limited period of time, because I want to get this new program off the ground
and really get it rolling." Or, "I've got this great program coming up, and I've got these
amazing bonuses that I've got for you. Now those bonuses are limited. So I've got a
limited registration period, but I'll share that with you in the next video." So you don't
want to be over the top and hammer people on this. It's just a one-liner, sort of offhand,
that it is a limited time period.
Here, I just give you some examples of ... These are ... A lot of these are really sort
of transition. Examples of how to say things and transition into the different parts of the
video.
In the beginning, "Thanks for all the comments. It's just fantastic hearing back

from so many of you, really love it." "Tons of energy from all your comments. Thanks,
keep it up." "In the last video I showed you," that's the recap. Do it quickly. "How I
discovered the answer," that's the recap. That's part of the recap of your authority
positioning. "What's possible?" That's a recap, the opportunity. "The success some of my
clients have been getting has been amazing." That can lead into a case study. "One of the
limiting beliefs, one of the most common questions people have has been ..." That's how
you walk into objections. "If you can do this, then this can or will happen." This is the
how, leading into the how. Remember this is the How video. "It's been awesome sharing
this information and seeing the excitement and results." This is leading into that soft
landing. "That's why I put together a training program, for the people that want to go
deeper." Again, that's part of the soft landing. "I'll be opening up the course next
Tuesday. I'm going to have limited seats in this, so be on the lookout for that email."
Then just a call for comments at the end is, "Please, scroll down, leave a comment, and
let me know what you think."
Okay, like I did with PLC1 and 2, I've got a checklist down here for you. So just
some things to think about as you go through this. So go out there and create your PLC3,
and just absolutely crush this.
